title: Review and setlist: Springsteen eviscerates Trump at fiery TD Garden stop
author: Peter Chianca
bullets: Bruce Springsteen criticized former President Trump during his 'No Kings' tour performance at TD Garden, May 24, 2026.
The concert featured 27 songs, including anthems like 'Born in the USA' and new political-inspired songs like 'Streets of Minneapolis.'
Guitarist Tom Morello joined Springsteen, adding to performances with notable songs like a cover of The Clash's 'Clampdown.'

title: Review and setlist: Leslie Odom Jr. brings Broadway to the Boston Pops
author: Shannon Murphy
bullets: Leslie Odom Jr. performed with the Boston Pops on May 22, marking his return after a decade.
The event celebrated American music, featuring works by Copland, Ellington, and a Hamilton medley.
Odom Jr. performed songs like 'Fly Me To The Moon' and 'Wait For It' with his band and the orchestra.

title: Chelsea man facing slew of assault charges for alleged violence against young children
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Kener Bautista-Zepeda, 19, from Chelsea, charged with a dozen assault-related offenses against two young children.
The charges include assault with a dangerous weapon, reckless endangerment, and suffocation; indictments followed disturbing recorded incidents.
Bautista-Zepeda was apprehended in Virginia; held without bail pending a May 28 dangerousness hearing.

title: Concord man, 73, convicted of raping 20-year-old in Sandwich restaurant bathroom in 2023
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Elie Jammal, 73, was convicted of raping a 20-year-old in a Sandwich restaurant in 2023 and sentenced to 13.5 to 15 years.
Jammal committed the assault in a restaurant hallway and bathroom after making unwanted advances toward the victim.
The Cape & Islands DA's Office said Jammal's actions were motivated by a 'shocking sense of entitlement.'

title: Newton Church, founded in 1893, is becoming condos
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Newton's Church of the Open Word at 19 Highland Ave will be converted into six condos with an additional building for 26 more.
The church, built in 1893, was closed in 2020 and purchased by Habitech Communities in 2024 for $4 million.
Plans to transform it into a community arts hub by the New Art Center fell through before the condo development approval.

title: 5 players the Celtics could target this offseason outside of Giannis Antetokounmpo
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Kawhi Leonard is a potential trade target for the Celtics, offering versatility and defensive prowess.
LeBron James, an unrestricted free agent, might join the Celtics if seeking a final title run.
John Collins, Jaxson Hayes, and Coby White provide options for forward and center depth or guard improvement.

title: 2 local college baseball programs advance to NCAA World Series
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Bentley University advances to NCAA Division II World Series for the first time by defeating Molloy University.
Endicott College wins its fourth consecutive Super Regional to qualify for NCAA Division III championship round.
Endicott's offensive surge includes three home runs in a decisive 12-4 Game 3 victory against Salve Regina.

title: Celtics reportedly plan to work out UConn basketball star ahead of NBA Draft
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: The Boston Celtics plan to work out UConn forward Alex Karaban next week ahead of the 2026 NBA Draft.
Alex Karaban, from Southborough, ranked No. 32 on ESPN’s top 100 prospects, completed four years at UConn.
Karaban finished his UConn career as the winningest player, holding records in games played, starts, and three-pointers made.

title: Review & setlist: Charlie Puth turns MGM Music Hall into a masterclass in musicianship
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Charlie Puth's 'Whatever's Clever! World Tour' electrified Boston's MGM Music Hall, showcasing his blend of jazz, pop, and nostalgia.
Puth's concert highlighted his musicianship, featuring live improvisations, note-perfect vocals, and engaging audience interactions.
Opening acts Ally Salort and Daniel Seavey impressed with indie-pop vocals and live looping, setting a high bar for the evening.

title: Review and setlist: Jakob Dylan’s choice to channel Tom Petty pays off at Beverly Wallflowers show
author: Peter Chianca
bullets: The Wallflowers performed 'Bringing Down the Horse' in full, followed by Tom Petty's 'Long After Dark' at The Cabot in Beverly.
The concert's highlight included hits like 'One Headlight' and a captivating rendition of 'Three Marlenas' showcasing Dylan's expressiveness.
Jakob Dylan and the Wallflowers concluded with Petty classics 'Refugee' and 'The Waiting,' energizing the sold-out crowd.
